title: Projection profile descriptor
description: Machine-readable per-projection profile contract describing how a governed projection fits a target surface. Shape + capability metadata ONLY — never permission, approval, eligibility, authority, secret, or canonical-state mutation. The egress mirror of the connector config descriptor (ADR-0015); see ADR-0019 and docs/PROJECTION_CONTRACT.md. Structural rules live here; semantic rules (id == target == family; authority-free discipline; required_canonical_ref/required_receipt enforcement) are enforced by scripts/validate_projection_profile.py.

target_surface.description: The surface this profile projects onto, e.g. 'issue', 'comment', 'channel'.

allowed_fields.description: Fields a projection of this kind MAY populate on the target surface.

forbidden_fields.description: Fields the projection MUST NOT touch (e.g. assignee, status-of-record, canonical-authority fields).

required_authority_label.description: The authority label the BOT must attach for this projection to be eligible — named here, decided bot-side. This is a declaration, not a grant.

required_canonical_ref.description: Whether the projection MUST carry a back-reference to the canonical decision it renders (anti-drift). True for all profiles.

required_receipt.description: Whether the external write MUST return a receipt mapped back to the bot. True for all mutating profiles.

rendering_constraints.description: Target rendering limits the renderer must respect.

max_length.description: Maximum character length for the primary rendered field.

max_title_length.description: Maximum character length for title fields (when applicable).

mutation_capability.description: The target operation this profile CAN drive — a declaration of target capability, NOT a grant of permission.

approval_class_hint.description: A non-binding hint to the bot's approval routing — never an approval itself.

reconciliation_capability.description: What reconciliation the target supports (e.g. update-in-place vs append-only) — capability metadata, not interpretation.
